title: 1: Code Maintainability

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 1.1: Adds fil_space_t::defragment() method for system tablespace defragmentation

annotations.description: Verifies that the fil_space_t::defragment() method is added to handle system tablespace defragmentation, callable during server operations.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch adds fil_space_t::defragment() method declaration in fil0fil.h and implementation in fsp0fsp.cc
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires adding defragmentation support for the InnoDB system tablespace. [5: Conclusion] An entry point for defragmentation is required to trigger the consolidation process.

title: 1.2: Performs each page move atomically within a single mini-transaction

annotations.description: Verifies that page moves (allocation, content copy, reference updates, deallocation) are performed atomically within a single mini-transaction so that a crash at any point leaves the tablespace in a consistent state.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch implements PageOperator class with prepare/complete pattern, executing all steps within one mtr before committing
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Each page move must be atomic'. [5: Conclusion] Atomic operations within a single mini-transaction ensure data integrity on crash.

title: 1.3: Restores original page state on failure during page relocation

annotations.description: Verifies that if an error occurs during page relocation, the implementation restores the original page state and discards modifications to maintain data integrity.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch implements save_old_page() to snapshot pages before modification and restore_old_pages() to revert them on error, followed by mtr->discard_modifications()
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Handle errors gracefully with rollback capability if something fails mid-operation'. [5: Conclusion] Rollback prevents partial updates from corrupting tablespace metadata.

title: 1.4: Updates all 3 B-tree references (parent child pointer, left sibling next pointer, right sibling previous pointer) when relocating a page

annotations.description: Verifies that when a B-tree page is relocated to a new page number, all 3 references to it are updated: the parent page's child pointer, the left sibling's FIL_PAGE_NEXT, and the right sibling's FIL_PAGE_PREV.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch implements AssociatedPages class with prepare() and complete() methods to fetch and update left/right/parent block references
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Relocate B-tree pages atomically, updating all references (parent, left sibling, right sibling)'. [5: Conclusion] All three reference types must be updated to maintain B-tree integrity.

title: 1.5: Invokes defragmentation during server restart, not shutdown

annotations.description: Verifies that defragmentation is triggered during server restart (startup) and not during shutdown, as explicitly required by the prompt.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch modifies fsp_system_tablespace_truncate() to call defragment() only when !shutdown parameter is true
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states 'Move data from later extents to earlier free extents during server restart (not during shutdown)'. [5: Conclusion] The timing constraint must be enforced.

title: 1.6: Skips defragmentation when user tables exist in system tablespace

annotations.description: Verifies that defragmentation is skipped if user tables exist in the system tablespace, and an informational message is logged suggesting to move them first.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch adds user_tables_exists() function and fil_space_t::defragment() returns early with sql_print_information message when user tables are found
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Skip defragmentation if user tables exist in the system tablespace (log a message suggesting to move them first)'. [5: Conclusion] User table detection and skip logic is required.

title: 1.7: Defragments all 7 system dictionary tables (SYS_TABLES, SYS_COLUMNS, SYS_INDEXES, SYS_FIELDS, SYS_FOREIGN, SYS_FOREIGN_COLS, SYS_VIRTUAL)

annotations.description: Verifies that defragmentation processes all 7 system dictionary tables.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch defragment_system_tables() method explicitly calls defragment_table() on dict_sys.sys_tables, dict_sys.sys_columns, dict_sys.sys_indexes, dict_sys.sys_fields, dict_sys.sys_foreign, dict_sys.sys_foreign_cols, and dict_sys.sys_virtual
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Only defragment system dictionary tables, not user tables'. [5: Conclusion] The scope must be limited to the 7 dictionary tables.

title: 1.8: Modifies flst_validate() to return dberr_t error codes instead of using assertions

annotations.description: Verifies that flst_validate() is modified to return dberr_t error codes (e.g., DB_CORRUPTION) instead of using ut_ad assertions, enabling graceful error handling in production.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch modifies flst_validate() signature to return dberr_t and replaces ut_ad assertions with return DB_CORRUPTION statements
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'return error codes instead of using assertions'. [5: Conclusion] Error codes enable proper error handling in release builds.

title: 1.9: Makes flst_validate() available in release builds by removing debug-only guards

annotations.description: Verifies that flst_validate() is available in release builds, not just debug builds, by removing the #ifdef UNIV_DEBUG / #endif guards around its declaration and definition.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes #ifdef UNIV_DEBUG and #endif guards around flst_validate in fut0lst.cc and fut0lst.h
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Make the existing list validation function available in release builds (not just debug)'. [5: Conclusion] Debug guards must be removed for release availability.

title: 1.10: Calls fsp_tablespace_validate() to validate tablespace free-space lists during the defragmentation flow

annotations.description: Verifies that fsp_tablespace_validate() is called within the defragmentation code path to validate the FSP header lists (FSP_FREE, FSP_FREE_FRAG, FSP_FULL_FRAG, FSP_SEG_INODES_FULL, FSP_SEG_INODES_FREE) and ensure tablespace integrity.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch calls fsp_tablespace_validate() within defragment_level() to validate these structures after page relocations
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Must validate data structures before and after operations'. [5: Conclusion] fsp_tablespace_validate() must be called in the defragmentation flow to ensure free-space list integrity.

title: 1.11: Validates index segment inode lists before and after defragmentation operations

annotations.description: Verifies that the segment inode lists (FSEG_FREE, FSEG_NOT_FULL, FSEG_FULL) are validated before and after defragmentation operations to ensure index segment integrity.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch calls fseg_validate_low() at key points during defragmentation to validate segment inode structures
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Must validate data structures before and after operations'. [5: Conclusion] Validation of segment inode lists ensures index operations maintain data integrity.

title: 3: Artifact Cleanup

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 3.1: Refactors xdes_get_state() to use uint32_t with combined declaration-assignment for the state variable

annotations.description: Verifies that xdes_get_state() is modernized: the separate 'ulint state; ... state = mach_read_from_4(...)' pattern is replaced with 'uint32_t state = mach_read_from_4(...)' — combining declaration with assignment while updating the type.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch changes 'ulint state; ... state = mach_read_from_4(...)' to 'uint32_t state = mach_read_from_4(...)' with matching return type
annotations.justification: [4: Qualitative Requirement] Cleaner code patterns improve readability. [5: Conclusion] Refactoring is an opportunity to clean up touched code.

title: 3.2: Tracks extent state to identify free and used extents

annotations.description: Verifies that a mechanism exists to track extent information (e.g., free vs used state) to identify which later extents can be moved to earlier free extents.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch implements SpaceDefragmenter class with m_extent_info map tracking extent states and m_extent_map for old-to-new extent mapping
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] The prompt requires moving data from later extents to earlier free extents; tracking extent state is required to identify which extents are free and which are used. [5: Conclusion] Extent state tracking enables the defragmentation algorithm.

title: 3.3: Traverses B-tree levels to relocate pages

annotations.description: Verifies that the implementation traverses B-tree index levels and relocates pages from later extents to earlier free extents while maintaining tree structure.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch implements IndexDefragmenter class with defragment_level() method that iterates through B-tree levels and relocates pages
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] The prompt requires relocating B-tree pages; traversing B-tree levels is required to find and move pages systematically. [5: Conclusion] Level-by-level traversal ensures all pages are considered for relocation.

title: 3.4: Adds dict_sys_t::is_sys_table() to distinguish system tables from user tables

annotations.description: Verifies that dict_sys_t::is_sys_table() is added to distinguish system dictionary tables from user tables when checking for user table presence.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch adds is_sys_table() method to dict_sys_t that checks table_id ranges
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] The user_tables_exists() check needs to distinguish system tables from user tables to implement the skip logic. [5: Conclusion] System table identification is required for the user table check.

title: 3.5: Implements atomic list manipulation for extent descriptor operations

annotations.description: Verifies that list manipulation operations (e.g., removing from one list, adding to another) for extent descriptors are performed atomically to ensure tablespace metadata consistency.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch implements flst namespace with remove_prepare/complete and append_prepare/complete functions
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] The prompt requires atomic page moves; extent descriptor list operations must also be atomic to maintain tablespace metadata consistency during relocation. [5: Conclusion] Atomic list operations prevent partial updates.

title: 4: Negative Rubrics

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 4.1: Breaks existing tablespace truncation logic in fsp_system_tablespace_truncate()

annotations.description: Detects if the implementation damages the existing tablespace truncation flow when adding defragmentation. The defragmentation must be integrated without breaking the existing shrink path.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch preserves the full existing garbage_collect() and truncation logic in fsp_system_tablespace_truncate(), adding defragment() as an additional step between garbage collection and extent traversal
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] The prompt asks to add defragmentation to improve the effectiveness of existing tablespace truncation. Breaking the existing truncation flow would be a regression. [5: Conclusion] Existing functionality must be preserved while adding defragmentation.
